pending.body: Update `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1448-1478` to use the new attempt-scoped reconciliation logic instead of `journal::last_terminal_event_type(&running_run_events)`. The helper added in `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:33-85` fixes resumed runs that crash after writing `run.json` but before durably appending `run_resumed`, but the core engine resume path still inspects the last terminal event for the whole `run_id` and can therefore inherit the previous attempt's `run_failed`/`run_completed`. Direct callers of `engine::resume_run_with_retry` can still misclassify that stale `Running` snapshot and either reject resume or reconcile it to the wrong terminal state. Add an engine-level regression test for this unjournaled-resume case.

The patch does not implement the requested lifecycle hardening at all: it only mutates metadata artifacts (`.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/run.json:1-74` and `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/journal.ndjson:195-208`) and contains no hunks in the required implementation files (`src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/run.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs`). Acceptance criteria items (pid tracking helpers, `run stop` command behavior, stale status detection, resume-side stale recovery, and SIGTERM-driven cleanup) are therefore not addressed in this change set.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 12 -> Plan and Implement
